task.title: DEADLINE_EXCEEDED error when indexing codebase - gRPC timeout during collection creation
task.description_markdown:
## Bug Description

When trying to index a codebase using the `index_codebase` tool, it consistently fails with a gRPC `DEADLINE_EXCEEDED` error during collection creation validation.

## Error Message

```
Error validating collection creation: 4 DEADLINE_EXCEEDED: Deadline exceeded after 15.001s, metadata filters: 0.001s, remote_addr=34.111.198.99:443
```

## Environment

- **MCP Package**: `@zilliz/claude-context-mcp@latest`
- **Embedding Provider**: VoyageAI (`voyage-code-3`)
- **Milvus Address**: Zilliz serverless cluster (`*.serverless.gcp-us-west1.cloud.zilliz.com`)
- **OS**: macOS Darwin 24.6.0
- **Claude Code**: CLI

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure `claude-context` MCP with a Zilliz serverless cluster
2. Call `index_codebase` with an absolute path to a local directory
3. Error is thrown immediately after 15 seconds

## Network Diagnostics

The Zilliz cluster endpoint is reachable:
- ✅ DNS resolves correctly
- ✅ TCP connection to port 443 succeeds
- ✅ TLS handshake completes successfully (TLSv1.3)
- ✅ Ping responds with ~22ms latency, 0% packet loss

The issue appears to be at the **gRPC layer** — the HTTP/HTTPS connection works fine, but the gRPC call times out during `collection creation validation`.

## Expected Behavior

The codebase should be indexed successfully.

## Actual Behavior

The tool times out after 15 seconds with `DEADLINE_EXCEEDED` on every attempt.

## Possible Cause

The default gRPC deadline of 15 seconds may be too short, especially when:
- The Zilliz serverless cluster is warming up / resuming from a paused state
- There is high latency on the gRPC metadata/collection API

## Suggested Fix

- Increase the gRPC deadline timeout for collection creation validation (e.g., 30–60 seconds)
- Add retry logic with exponential backoff for transient timeout errors
- Provide a clearer error message indicating the cluster may be paused/warming up
task.author: aud1ence

comments.title: aud1ence
comments.content:
## Related Issues

Found several existing issues reporting the same or similar problem:

### Directly related (same error)
- **#289** — [DEADLINE_EXCEEDED on checkCollectionLimit() - gRPC timeout on collection creation (Free tier, macOS)](https://github.com/zilliztech/claude-context/issues/289) *(opened 1 day ago)*
  - Identical error on the same remote addr `34.111.198.99:443`
  - Root cause traced to `checkCollectionLimit()` in `milvus-vectordb.js:607` using a dummy create/drop collection as a pre-flight check with no configurable timeout
  - Suggests replacing with `listCollections()` + count to avoid heavy gRPC call

- **#215** — [Indexing error : Error validating collection creation](https://github.com/zilliztech/claude-context/issues/215) *(open)*
  - Same error: `Error validating collection creation`

### Suggested fix (from #289)
The root cause is `checkCollectionLimit()` creating a dummy collection to test limits, which triggers a gRPC call that exceeds the hardcoded 15s deadline. Fix options:
1. **Configurable timeout** via env var (e.g. `MILVUS_TIMEOUT_MS`)
2. **Replace dummy collection check** with `listCollections()` + count (less expensive)
3. **REST API fallback** — `MilvusRestfulVectorDatabase` exists but isn't wired as fallback

linked_tasks.title: DEADLINE_EXCEEDED on checkCollectionLimit() - gRPC timeout on collection creation (Free tier, macOS)
linked_tasks.content:
## Bug Description

Consistent `DEADLINE_EXCEEDED` error when attempting to index a codebase. The error occurs during the pre-flight `checkCollectionLimit()` call before any actual indexing begins. A different codebase on the same cluster was successfully indexed 4 days prior.

## Environment

- **OS**: macOS Darwin 25.3.0 (Apple Silicon)
- **Node.js**: v24.3.0
- **Package**: `@zilliz/claude-context-mcp@0.1.6`
- **Zilliz Cloud**: Free tier, `gcp-us-west1`, Milvus 2.6.x compatible
- **Cluster**: `in03-e63f077fc4f1b69`

## Cluster State

- **Collections**: 1 of 5 allowed (`hybrid_code_chunks_9429127a`, status: Loaded)
- **Storage**: 0.01 GB of 5 GB
- **Read vCUs**: ~12 (minimal load)
- No resource limits hit

## Error

```
Error validating collection creation: 4 DEADLINE_EXCEEDED: Deadline exceeded after 15.001s,remote_addr=34.111.198.99:443
```

## Root Cause Trace

The error originates in `milvus-vectordb.js:607` (`checkCollectionLimit()`), which creates a dummy collection as a pre-flight limit check:

```javascript
async checkCollectionLimit() {
    const collectionName = `dummy_collection_${Date.now()}`;
    // creates a trivial 2-field collection, then drops it
    await this.client.createCollection(createCollectionParams);  // <-- times out here
}
```

This is called from `handlers.js:249` before any real indexing starts:

```javascript
const canCreateCollection = await this.context.getVectorDatabase().checkCollectionLimit();
```

The `MilvusClient` at `milvus-vectordb.js:20` is created with no `timeout` parameter, so the 15s gRPC deadline is the SDK default.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Have an existing indexed codebase (collection loaded and working)
2. Attempt to index a second, different codebase: `index_codebase({ path: "/path/to/project" })`
3. Consistently fails with `DEADLINE_EXCEEDED` on the `checkCollectionLimit()` dummy collection creation
4. Tried with both `ast` and `langchain` splitters, with and without `force: true`, with and without `ignorePatterns` — all produce the same error
5. Indexing a subdirectory also fails identically

## Key Observation

- Another codebase (`~/gaylonphotos`, 297 files, 1241 chunks) was successfully indexed on this same cluster on Mar 22, 2026
- The failing codebase (`~/BTC-dashboard`) was previously indexed (196k chunks, Feb 20) but was cleared during a force-reindex attempt
- Since then, every attempt to create any new collection times out
- `get_indexing_status` confirms the codebase is not indexed
- `clear_index` confirms nothing to clear

## Related

- Issue #154 — same `DEADLINE_EXCEEDED` error, also on Node v24.x
- Issue #126 — similar collection creation failure (different error format)

## Suggested Improvements

1. **Configurable gRPC timeout** — allow setting a timeout > 15s via env var (e.g., `MILVUS_TIMEOUT_MS`)
2. **Skip dummy collection check** — `checkCollectionLimit()` could use `listCollections()` + count instead of create/drop, avoiding the gRPC-heavy collection creation path
3. **REST API fallback** — `MilvusRestfulVectorDatabase` exists in `claude-context-core` but isn't wired up as a fallback when gRPC fails

children.title: MCP Fails when started to index the codebase
Error validating collection creation during indexing
children.content:
## Bug Description
Consistent indexing failure with error message: `Error validating collection creation: [object Object]`

## Environment
- **OS**: macOS (Darwin 24.5.0, ARM64)
- **Node.js**: v22.12.0
- **Package Manager**: pnpm 10.11.0
- **Codebase Size**: ~8.2GB
- **Architecture**: arm64 (Apple Silicon)

## Reproduction Steps
1. Attempt to index codebase using `mcp__code-context__index_codebase`
2. Try with different parameters:
   - Both `ast` and `langchain` splitters
   - With and without `force: true`
   - With and without custom extensions
   - Different path formats

## Error Details
- **Consistent Error**: `Error validating collection creation: [object Object]`
- **Occurs**: During initial collection creation phase
- **Frequency**: 100% reproduction rate across all parameter combinations
- **No Stack Trace**: Only the generic error object is returned

## Attempted Solutions
- Cleared existing index with `clear_index`
- Tried both splitter types (`ast`, `langchain`)
- Used force parameter to overwrite any existing data
- Verified directory exists and has proper read permissions
- Tested with absolute path (required parameter)

## Additional Context
Error appears similar to issue #113 but with different specific error message. The `[object Object]` suggests an error object is not being properly serialized/displayed, making debugging difficult.

Would appreciate guidance on:
1. How to get more detailed error information
2. Any known compatibility issues with the specified environment
3. Workarounds or alternative approaches

linked_tasks.title: Indexing error : Error validating collection creation
linked_tasks.content:
**Describe the bug**
Impossible to index or reindex codebase.

**Software version:**
 - Claude Code latest
 
**Additional context**
```
    "claude-context": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "@zilliz/claude-context-mcp@latest"
      ],
      "env": {
        "OPENAI_API_KEY": "sk-xxx",
        "MILVUS_TOKEN": "xxx"
      }
    }
```
```
> index codebase

⏺ I'll index the codebase to enable semantic search. This will help me understand the project structure and
  find relevant code when needed.

⏺ claude-context - index_codebase (MCP)(path:
                                       "/Users/AAA/xxx")
  ⎿  Error: Error validating collection creation: [object Object]
```
